heading: Understatement of taxpayer’s liability by tax return preparer
label: 6694 Understatement of taxpayer’s liability by tax return preparer
notes: CodificationAnother section 6694, relating to failure to file information with respect to carryover basis property, which was added by , was renumbered section 6698 by , renumbered section 6698A by , and repealed by .Pub. L. 94–455, § 2005(d)(2)Pub. L. 95–600Pub. L. 96–222Pub. L. 96–223

Amendments2015—Subsec. (b)(1)(B). substituted “75 percent” for “50 percent”.Pub. L. 114–1132008—Subsec. (a). amended subsec. (a) generally. Prior to amendment, subsec. (a) provided penalty for understatement due to unreasonable positions.Pub. L. 110–3432007—, substituted “tax return preparer” for “income tax return preparer” in section catchline.Pub. L. 110–28, § 8246(a)(2)(F)(i)(I)Subsec. (a). , amended heading and text of subsec. (a) generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “If—Pub. L. 110–28, § 8246(b)“(1) any part of any understatement of liability with respect to any return or claim for refund is due to a position for which there was not a realistic possibility of being sustained on its merits,“(2) any person who is an income tax return preparer with respect to such return or claim knew (or reasonably should have known) of such position, and“(3) such position was not disclosed as provided in section 6662(d)(2)(B)(ii) or was frivolous,such person shall pay a penalty of $250 with respect to such return or claim unless it is shown that there is reasonable cause for the understatement and such person acted in good faith.”Subsec. (b). , amended heading and text of subsec. (b) generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “If any part of any understatement of liability with respect to any return or claim for refund is due—Pub. L. 110–28, § 8246(b)“(1) to a willful attempt in any manner to understate the liability for tax by a person who is an income tax return preparer with respect to such return or claim, or“(2) to any reckless or intentional disregard of rules or regulations by any such person,such person shall pay a penalty of $1,000 with respect to such return or claim. With respect to any return or claim, the amount of the penalty payable by any person by reason of this subsection shall be reduced by the amount of the penalty paid by such person by reason of subsection (a).”Subsec. (c)(1). , substituted “a tax return preparer” for “an income tax return preparer”.Pub. L. 110–28, § 8246(a)(2)(F)(i)(II)Subsec. (c)(2). , substituted “the tax return preparer” for “the income tax return preparer”.Pub. L. 110–28, § 8246(a)(2)(F)(i)(III)Subsec. (e). , substituted “this title” for “subtitle A”.Pub. L. 110–28, § 8246(a)(2)(F)(i)(IV)Subsec. (f). , substituted “tax return preparer” for “income tax return preparer”.Pub. L. 110–28, § 8246(a)(2)(F)(i)(V)1989—Subsec. (a). , substituted “Understatements due to unrealistic positions” for “Negligent or intentional disregard of rules and regulations” in heading and amended text generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “If any part of any understatement of liability with respect to any return or claim for refund is due to the negligent or intentional disregard of rules and regulations by any person who is an income tax return preparer with respect to such return or claim, such person shall pay a penalty of $100 with respect to such return or claim.”Pub. L. 101–239, § 7732(a)Subsec. (b). , substituted “Willful or reckless conduct” for “Willful understatement of liability” in heading and amended text generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “If any part of any understatement of liability with respect to any return or claim for refund is due to a willful attempt in any manner to understate the liability for a tax by a person who is an income tax return preparer with respect to such return or claim, such person shall pay a penalty of $500 with respect to such return or claim. With respect to any return or claim, the amount of the penalty payable by any person by reason of this subsection shall be reduced by the amount of the penalty paid by such person by reason of subsection (a).”Pub. L. 101–239, § 7732(a)Subsec. (c)(1). , inserted at end “Nothing in this paragraph shall be construed to prohibit any counterclaim for the remainder of such penalty in a proceeding begun as provided in paragraph (2).”Pub. L. 101–239, § 7737(a)

children.heading: Understatement due to unreasonable positions
children.label: a Understatement due to unreasonable positions
A
children.text: prepares any return or claim of refund with respect to which any part of an understatement of liability is due to a position described in paragraph (2), and

children.label: B
children.text: knew (or reasonably should have known) of the position,

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Except as otherwise provided in this paragraph, a position is described in this paragraph unless there is or was substantial authority for the position.

children.heading: Disclosed positions
children.label: B Disclosed positions
children.text: If the position was disclosed as provided in section 6662(d)(2)(B)(ii)(I) and is not a position to which subparagraph (C) applies, the position is described in this paragraph unless there is a reasonable basis for the position.

children.heading: Tax shelters and reportable transactions
children.label: C Tax shelters and reportable transactions
children.text: If the position is with respect to a tax shelter (as defined in section 6662(d)(2)(C)(ii)) or a reportable transaction to which section 6662A applies, the position is described in this paragraph unless it is reasonable to believe that the position would more likely than not be sustained on its merits.

children.heading: Reasonable cause exception
children.label: 3 Reasonable cause exception
children.text: No penalty shall be imposed under this subsection if it is shown that there is reasonable cause for the understatement and the tax return preparer acted in good faith.

children.heading: Understatement due to willful or reckless conduct
children.label: b Understatement due to willful or reckless conduct
B
children.text: 75 percent of the income derived (or to be derived) by the tax return preparer with respect to the return or claim.

children.label: A
children.text: a willful attempt in any manner to understate the liability for tax on the return or claim, or

children.label: B
children.text: a reckless or intentional disregard of rules or regulations.

children.heading: Reduction in penalty
children.label: 3 Reduction in penalty
children.text: The amount of any penalty payable by any person by reason of this subsection for any return or claim for refund shall be reduced by the amount of the penalty paid by such person by reason of subsection (a).

children.heading: Extension of period of collection where preparer pays 15 percent of penalty
children.label: c Extension of period of collection where preparer pays 15 percent of penalty

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: If, within 30 days after the day on which notice and demand of any penalty under subsection (a) or (b) is made against any person who is a tax return preparer, such person pays an amount which is not less than 15 percent of the amount of such penalty and files a claim for refund of the amount so paid, no levy or proceeding in court for the collection of the remainder of such penalty shall be made, begun, or prosecuted until the final resolution of a proceeding begun as provided in paragraph (2). Notwithstanding the provisions of section 7421(a), the beginning of such proceeding or levy during the time such prohibition is in force may be enjoined by a proceeding in the proper court. Nothing in this paragraph shall be construed to prohibit any counterclaim for the remainder of such penalty in a proceeding begun as provided in paragraph (2).

children.heading: Preparer must bring suit in district court to determine his liability for penalty
children.label: 2 Preparer must bring suit in district court to determine his liability for penalty
children.text: If, within 30 days after the day on which his claim for refund of any partial payment of any penalty under subsection (a) or (b) is denied (or, if earlier, within 30 days after the expiration of 6 months after the day on which he filed the claim for refund), the tax return preparer fails to begin a proceeding in the appropriate United States district court for the determination of his liability for such penalty, paragraph (1) shall cease to apply with respect to such penalty, effective on the day following the close of the applicable 30-day period referred to in this paragraph.

children.heading: Suspension of running of period of limitations on collection
children.label: 3 Suspension of running of period of limitations on collection
children.text: The running of the period of limitations provided in section 6502 on the collection by levy or by a proceeding in court in respect of any penalty described in paragraph (1) shall be suspended for the period during which the Secretary is prohibited from collecting by levy or a proceeding in court.

children.heading: Abatement of penalty where taxpayer’s liability not understated
children.label: d Abatement of penalty where taxpayer’s liability not understated
children.text: If at any time there is a final administrative determination or a final judicial decision that there was no understatement of liability in the case of any return or claim for refund with respect to which a penalty under subsection (a) or (b) has been assessed, such assessment shall be abated, and if any portion of such penalty has been paid the amount so paid shall be refunded to the person who made such payment as an overpayment of tax without regard to any period of limitations which, but for this subsection, would apply to the making of such refund.

children.heading: Understatement of liability defined
children.label: e Understatement of liability defined
children.text: For purposes of this section, the term “understatement of liability” means any understatement of the net amount payable with respect to any tax imposed by this title or any overstatement of the net amount creditable or refundable with respect to any such tax. Except as otherwise provided in subsection (d), the determination of whether or not there is an understatement of liability shall be made without regard to any administrative or judicial action involving the taxpayer.

children.heading: Cross reference
children.label: f Cross reference
children.text: For definition of tax return preparer, see section 7701(a)(36).
